changes.rationale: Adds column to high-traffic payments table. Migration is additive (ADD COLUMN with DEFAULT) so non-locking on PostgreSQL 11+, but retry_count DEFAULT 0 triggers a full table rewrite on PostgreSQL <11. Verify server version.
changes.notes: Column with DEFAULT is an online operation in PG11+. If PG<11, this locks the table for the duration of the rewrite.

changes.rationale: Struct field addition. Go struct tags match DB column. No behavior change in model itself.
Adds retry decision logic: if retry_count >= 3, mark as failed instead of retrying. Threshold is hardcoded (not configurable). Affects payment completion rate.
Modified SQL queries to include retry_count in SELECT and UPDATE. Uses sqlc-generated code. INCREMENT operation uses SET retry_count = retry_count + 1 (safe for concurrent access).
changes.notes: Rollback requires reverting queries AND ensuring old code doesn't SELECT the new column (it won't — Go ignores unknown columns with sqlc defaults).

changes.rationale: Adds retry_count to GET /api/v1/payments/:id response. Additive field — non-breaking for clients that ignore unknown fields. OpenAPI spec updated to match.
Documents new field. Spec change only — no runtime impact.

compound_changes.description: Schema migration + business logic + API response
compound_changes.rationale: The service.go logic depends on the new column existing. If you rollback the migration but not the code, service.go will fail on SELECT. Must rollback in order: code first, then migration.
